reference: My grandmother is in critical condition right now. She is on dialysis due to end-stage renal failure, has experienced four heart attacks, septicemia, and a hemorrhagic stroke. Today, she is making voluntary movements while on a ventilator. How come the doctor is still administering dialysis to her? Please explain why.
text: My grandmother is in critical condition right now. She was on dialysis due to the end-stage spinal failure. She has experienced four heart attacks, septicemia, and a hemorrhagic stroke. Today, she is making a role in her immune response while on the rotation. How can the doctor still administering dialysis to her? It is a plain line.
